page.content: The following table presents data from the Beck Depression Inventory (BDI-13), which is utilized to assess the severity of depressive symptoms in a given population. The BDI-13 encompasses various items that collectively measure aspects of mood, cognition, and behavioral changes associated with depression. Notably, the metrics of "Mean" and "Standard Deviation (SD)" are provided for each item, allowing for a quantitative evaluation of respondents' experiences. For instance, the item relating to depressed mood or sadness recorded a mean score of 1.24 with a standard deviation of 1.03, indicating a moderate level of reported sadness among participants and a variability in responses. This data is essential for understanding the prevalence and intensity of depressive symptoms within the surveyed group. Additionally, the metrics allow stakeholders to identify areas requiring further intervention and support, thereby facilitating targeted strategies for mental health improvement. Overall, the data highlights the importance of continuous monitoring of depressive symptoms, as well as the need for comprehensive treatment options tailored to the unique experiences of individuals affected by depression. This contextual framework is vital for interpreting the data and implementing effective mental health initiatives.

reported sadness among participants and a variability in responses. This data is essential for understanding the prevalence and

page.html:
BDI-13 itemsMean (SD)
(1) Depressed mood/sadness1.24 (1.03)
(2) Pessimism.54 (.79)
(3) Sense of failure1.15 (.98)
(4) Lack of satisfaction/dissatisfaction.96 (.97)
(5) Guilty feelings.83 (1.03)
(6) Self-hate/self-dislike.64 (.85)
(7) Self-punitive wishes/self-harm.39 (.90)
(8) Social withdrawal.83 (.91)
(9) Indecisiveness1.24 (.85)
(10) Distorted body image/self-image.69 (1.03)
(11) Work inhibition/work difficulty.92 (.99)
(12) Fatigability.98 (.84)
(13) Loss of appetite.43 (.69)
Total score11.03 (7.55)
